objective: Train GaugeAnything-owned measurement specialists from accumulated physical-quantity datasets, while keeping foundation backbones optional and frozen.
principles: Do not claim a new foundation model; claim a measurement specialist unless scale and data justify more.
Every learned head must beat the strongest simple baseline on held-out sources.
Report coverage/gating and uncertainty, not only successful measurements.
Separate WHERE quality (mask/localization) from HOW-MUCH quality (physical quantity regression).
Prefer license-clean physical GT for headline training; keep unclear datasets in research-only buckets.

external_backbone_options.name: SAM family
external_backbone_options.use: frozen teacher or pseudo-label generator; optional LoRA only after owned heads beat calibration baselines
external_backbone_options.risk: gated or separate licenses; masks are not measurement-ready for thin/fuzzy/field regimes

external_backbone_options.name: DINOv2 / self-supervised ViT features
external_backbone_options.role: license/weight-permitting visual encoder for small measurement heads
external_backbone_options.use: frozen feature extractor for Base/Mid models
external_backbone_options.risk: features are generic; must prove measurement transfer

external_backbone_options.name: Depth Anything / metric depth families
external_backbone_options.use: depth token or teacher signal for ADT/TUM/ARKit scenes
external_backbone_options.risk: metric depth error can dominate measurement unless scale gates are explicit

external_backbone_options.name: open-world counting models
external_backbone_options.role: comparison baseline for rebar/log/fish count heads
external_backbone_options.use: benchmark reference, not necessarily training dependency
external_backbone_options.risk: counting output alone lacks spacing/diameter/physical measurement grammar

owned_model_families.description: Task heads over frozen masks, profiles, image statistics, or small CNNs.
owned_model_families.release_rule: Can ship per-head if it beats calibration/classical baselines and license is clean.

owned_model_families.description: Frozen encoder plus prompt/regime/scale tokens and multi-task measurement heads.
owned_model_families.release_rule: Must improve at least three physical-quantity families without hurting per-domain worst case.

owned_model_families.description: LoRA/adapters on a vision encoder with text/regime tokens; trained on real + synthetic physical GT.
owned_model_families.release_rule: Requires held-out dataset-family generalization, not just random splits.

owned_model_families.description: Prompt-conditioned physical measurement model distilled from SAM/depth/count teachers plus real GT.
owned_model_families.release_rule: Only justified after Base/Mid show scaling law; otherwise it is compute theater.

training_atoms.task: SAM mask/image -> crack width from mask-derived GT
training_atoms.next_model: GaugeHead-Tiny now narrowly beats quantile calibration; next target is reducing CrackTree200 worst-source error and calibrated uncertainty.
training_atoms.license_note: research-only until CrackSeg9k subset licenses are separated

training_atoms.next_model: scale-normalized profile CNN + uncertainty + image-level localization gate
training_atoms.license_note: CC BY 4.0; cleanest headline training atom

training_atoms.next_model: regime router with uncertainty; do not overclaim learned field head before beating classical residual
training_atoms.license_note: research-only until MT license is clarified

training_atoms.next_model: density/centroid head trained from instance masks; target MAE < 5 on held-out scenes
detected/SAM document quad model; replace GT quad
training_atoms.license_note: check SmartDoc redistribution terms before release

training_atoms.task: RGB-D/video -> stable scale or object dimensions
training_atoms.next_model: replace oracle object gate with prompt/segmentation gate

benchmark_rules: Primary splits must be source/dataset-family held out, not random image splits.
Each model row must include simple baseline, previous neural baseline, new model, coverage, and worst-domain.
A learned model that loses to a calibration or classical baseline is kept as negative/reproducibility, not headline.
Report units as physical units where possible; mark px-derived GT separately.
Save checkpoints and result JSONs for every trained artifact.
